stars.description: Universal Rust multiplexer with a typed SDK — drive any CLI or TUI app from code. Native on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
stars.highlights: rmux is a universal Rust multiplexer that allows you to programmatically drive any CLI or TUI application via a typed SDK. It supports native execution on Linux, macOS, and Windows, making it a cross-platform tool for automating terminal interactions.
stars.worthReading: For AI engineers building agentic systems that need to interact with CLI tools, rmux provides a robust, type-safe way to control terminals programmatically, which is a common requirement in agent frameworks.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is moderate (567 stars, rising but not explosive), relevance is high for AI agents needing CLI control (agent/tooling), and concreteness is strong (working code with typed SDK).

stars.description: Pi coding agent extension: llama.cpp provider with dynamic model + context window discovery
stars.highlights: Pi-llama is a coding agent extension that integrates llama.cpp as a provider, enabling dynamic model and context window discovery. It allows users to leverage local LLMs for coding tasks with flexible model selection and automatic context size adjustment.
stars.worthReading: This repo is worth exploring for developers interested in running local coding agents with customizable LLM backends, especially those using llama.cpp for on-device inference.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is high (new repo with low stars but clear traction from a Hugging Face team member), relevance is high (coding agent + LLM integration), concreteness is moderate (working extension but early stage).

stars.highlights: Teich is a Python library for building and managing AI agents with a focus on modularity and extensibility. It provides tools for agent orchestration, memory management, and tool integration, aiming to simplify the development of complex AI workflows.
stars.worthReading: As a new entrant in the agent-building space, Teich offers a fresh perspective on modular agent design, which could be valuable for developers looking to experiment with agent architectures.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is moderate (low stars but new, no clear traction yet; 2), relevance is high (agent building is frontier AI; 3), concreteness is low (no description, minimal info; 1), total 6, but clamped to 4 due to lack of evidence of real artifact.

stars.description: Open Source Robotic Arm for All Developers
stars.highlights: reBot-DevArm is an open-source robotic arm designed for developers, offering a platform for experimentation and learning in robotics. It provides hardware schematics, firmware, and software tools to enable custom control and integration with AI systems.
stars.worthReading: This repo bridges the gap between software and hardware, making it a valuable resource for AI developers interested in embodied AI and robotics applications.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is moderate (3.5k stars, not extremely new but gaining traction), relevance is high (embodied AI frontier), and concreteness is strong (working hardware/software artifact).

stars.description: AI agent skills published by NVIDIA
stars.highlights: NVIDIA's curated collection of reusable AI agent skills, designed to accelerate development of agentic workflows. Provides modular, production-ready building blocks for common agent tasks.
stars.worthReading: Offers a practical, vendor-backed library of agent skills that can be directly integrated into projects, saving development time and leveraging NVIDIA's expertise.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is moderate (259 stars, new repo but not explosive growth), relevance is high (directly about AI agents and tooling), concreteness is high (specific skills with code).
